# 🐛 Bug Report Template

**Use this template when you encounter unexpected insects at a "bug-free" destination.**

---

## Destination

- **Location:** [e.g., "North coast of Tenerife, Canary Islands"]
- **Month/Date of visit:** [e.g., "March 2025"]
- **Bug Score at time of visit:** [e.g., "4/10 — much worse than expected"]

---

## What You Found

- **Bug type(s):** [e.g., "mosquitoes, sandflies, centipedes"]
- **Severity:** [e.g., "swarming at dawn near a lagoon; occasional in town center"]
- **Expected vs Actual:** [e.g., "Guide said 0/10 for this month; found them near water"]

---

## Location Details

- **Specific area:** [e.g., "Playa Jardín beach front, near the aquarium"]
- **Approximate time of day:** [e.g., "dawn around 6:30–7am"]
- **Weather conditions that day:** [e.g., "cool, light breeze, humid"]
- **Notes:** Were you near standing water? Near vegetation? Near other tourists' pools?

---

## Suggested Update (if you have an idea)

- **Suggested revision:** [e.g., "Update Curaçao guide: add warning about eastern coast mangrove areas in wet season"]
- **Proposed bug score revision:** [e.g., "From 2/10 to 3/10 for this specific area in May–Jul"]

---

## Sources/Proof (optional but helpful)

- **Photos:** [link to photo or attachment]
- **Local forum/report links:** [link to TripAdvisor bug forum, etc.]

---

> ⚠️ **Thank you!** Bug sightings help keep our data accurate for the whole community. Climate change is shifting insect ranges — your reports are critical!
